segments.text: Blessed be the Lord my Rock, Who trains my hands for war, And my fingers for battle—
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My lovingkindness and my fortress, My high tower and my deliverer, My shield and the One in whom I take refuge, Who subdues my people under me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Lord, what is man, that You take knowledge of him? Or the son of man, that You are mindful of him?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Man is like a breath; His days are like a passing shadow.
Bow down Your heavens, O Lord, and come down; Touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; Shoot out Your arrows and destroy them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Stretch out Your hand from above; Rescue me and deliver me out of great waters, From the hand of foreigners,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Whose mouth speaks lying words, And whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
I will sing a new song to You, O God; On a harp of ten strings I will sing praises to You,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The One who gives salvation to kings, Who delivers David His servant From the deadly sword.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of foreigners, Whose mouth speaks lying words, And whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood—
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: That our sons may be as plants grown up in their youth; That our daughters may be as pillars, Sculptured in palace style;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: That our barns may be full, Supplying all kinds of produce; That our sheep may bring forth thousands And ten thousands in our fields;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: That our oxen may be well laden; That there be no breaking in or going out; That there be no outcry in our streets.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Happy are the people who are in such a state; Happy are the people whose God is the Lord!
segments.speaker: David
